prompt: I've got test scores for 200 students across 15 schools. I want to understand how much of the variation is between schools vs within schools, and get school-level estimates that account for different sample sizes. Some schools only have 5 students.
assertions: Builds a hierarchical/multilevel model with school-level and student-level parameters
Uses non-centered parameterization (mu_offset * sigma pattern or equivalent)
Uses az.summary() or az.plot_trace() for convergence diagnostics
Includes shrinkage analysis showing small schools pulled toward grand mean
Computes ICC (intraclass correlation) or variance partition coefficient
Uses coords and dims in the PyMC model definition
Reports credible intervals (94% HDI preferred) for school-level estimates
